hero.subtitle: Language integration for professionals who are tired of being treated like tourists in their own city.

philosophy.label: The Methodology
philosophy.title: The textbook
philosophy.intro: Most courses teach you how to pass an exam. We teach you how to handle a doctor's appointment, argue with a landlord, and make friends at a bar.

item1.title: Context over Grammar
item1.text: We don't just drill cases. We simulate real-life scenarios—bureaucracy, medical visits, social events—so you're ready when they actually happen.

item2.title: Cultural Fluency
item2.text: Speaking Czech isn't just about words. It's about knowing the unwritten rules of the society. When to be formal, when to push back, and when to joke.

item3.title: Community First
item3.text: Isolation is the enemy of integration. Our students become a network. We organize hikes, coffees, and events to keep you connected.

leadMagnet.description1: Most courses teach you grammar tables. Locals don't speak in tables.
leadMagnet.description3: : 50 essential phrases to soften requests, fill pauses, and sound like you actually live here.

items.q: How do I know my level?
items.a: If you can handle basics in shops but freeze in conversation, A2 or B1 is typical. We confirm your level during onboarding.

items.q: How many students are in a cohort?
Can I switch cohorts if my schedule changes?
items.a: Yes, if there is space in another cohort. We’ll help you reschedule.
We offer a full refund within 7 days before the cohort starts.

cheatSheet.title: Natural Czech Cheat Sheet
cheatSheet.subtitle: 50 Essential Phrases Locals Actually Use
cheatSheet.tagline: Stop sounding like a textbook. Start sounding like you live here.
cheatSheet.introBold: Grammar tables won't help you at the pub.
cheatSheet.introBody1: This cheat sheet gives you the phrases Czechs use every single day—the ones that make you sound natural, not like you're reading from a language app.
cheatSheet.introBody2: Master these 50 phrases and you'll handle small talk, soften requests, fill awkward pauses, and actually understand what people say on the street.
cheatSheet.proTipBody: Don't try to memorize all 50 at once. Pick 5-10 phrases that fit situations you encounter daily. Use them until they feel automatic, then add more. The goal isn't perfection—it's sounding natural and feeling confident in real conversations.
cheatSheet.footerTitle: Want to sound even more natural?
cheatSheet.footerBody: This cheat sheet is just the beginning. Our courses teach you how to actually USE these phrases in real conversations, understand responses, and build genuine fluency.

phrases.context: More casual than "Jak se máš?" Use with friends, colleagues.
Perfect for catching up with someone you haven't seen in a while.
The go-to Czech response. Modest and real.
Classic Czech understatement. Never too enthusiastic!
When you're flexible about plans or choices.
Way softer than commanding. "Mohl bys mi pomoct?" = Could you help me?
Easy, friendly way to agree or reassure someone.
Super polite. "Nevadilo by ti otevřít okno?" = Would you mind opening the window?
Add this to any request to sound polite. "Dáš mi to, prosím tě?"
Warmer than plain "Děkuji." Use it everywhere.
The ultimate filler. Start sentences, pause, think. Czechs say this constantly.
NOT "no"! It's like "well" or a thinking sound. "No, nevím..." = Well, I don't know...
When you're correcting or reconsidering. "Vlastně, mám čas." = Actually, I have time.
Younger Czechs use this all the time to clarify or emphasize.
Buys you time when thinking. Very natural.
"Prostě nevím" = I just don't know. Adds emphasis.
Reassuring, chill. "V pohodě" = relaxed, no worries.
When you're impressed or excited.
Can be positive or negative depending on tone.
Express surprise or disbelief.
Show sympathy when someone shares bad news.
"Bohužel nemůžu" = Unfortunately I can't. Very common.

pub.title: At the Pub, Restaurant, or Shop

phrases.context: "Dám si pivo" = I'll have a beer. Standard ordering phrase.
At the pub when you want another round.
How to ask for the bill at a restaurant.
Essential for modern transactions.
Toasting with drinks OR responding to a sneeze!
Say this before eating or to others who are eating.
Basic but essential. "Kde je metro?" = Where's the metro?
Polite way to ask for directions from strangers.
Getting someone's attention or apologizing politely.
On crowded trams or in shops when you need to pass.
When running late or someone's waiting for you.
Informal, common among young people. "To je fakt dobré!" = That's really good!
Agreeing enthusiastically. Very common.
Agreeing or showing you're listening. Drawn out.
Added at the end for confirmation. "Je to dobrý, né?" = It's good, right?
Super casual. Everything's chill.
Agreeing with someone's point.
When you understand and agree with reasoning.
Confirming plans. Very satisfying to say!
"Sejdeme se v šest" = Let's meet at six.

phrases.meaning: I'll be in touch. / I'll call/text.
phrases.context: When ending a conversation and planning to reconnect.
Polite way to exit a conversation.

phrases.meaning: I'm keeping my fingers crossed for you. / Good luck!
phrases.context: Literal: "I'm holding thumbs." Way to wish someone luck.
Casual, friendly goodbye. "Měj se hezky!" = Have a nice one!

phrases.meaning: We'll see each other. / See you.
phrases.context: Standard parting phrase when you'll meet again.
Casual, friendly. Perfect for people you see regularly.
Polite parting. Shopkeepers say this all the time.
Short for "na shledanou." More casual.

sections.heading: 1. Company Information
paragraphs: These terms and conditions govern your use of services provided by Jazyk a Integrace s.r.o., a company incorporated under Czech law.
Company details: Jazyk a Integrace s.r.o., IČO: 23812036, registered at Městský soud v Praze, section C, file 433136, with registered office at Černomořská 384/9, 101 00 Praha 10, Czech Republic.

sections.heading: 2. Services
paragraphs: We provide Czech language education services including online courses, in-person instruction, and related educational materials.
Course schedules, formats, and content may be adjusted based on enrollment and pedagogical requirements. We reserve the right to cancel courses with insufficient enrollment with full refund.

sections.heading: 3. Registration and Account
paragraphs: To access our services, you must create an account with accurate, current, and complete information.
You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities under your account.
You must be at least 18 years old or have parental consent to use our services.

sections.heading: 4. Pricing and Payment
paragraphs: Prices are displayed in EUR and include Czech VAT where applicable. Payments are processed securely through Stripe.
Full payment is required to secure enrollment. We accept credit cards, debit cards, and other payment methods available through our payment processor.
Currency conversion fees, if any, are your responsibility.

sections.heading: 5. Consumer Rights and Withdrawal
paragraphs: As a consumer, you have the right to withdraw from the contract within 14 days of purchase without stating a reason, in accordance with Czech Civil Code.
However, if you expressly consent to the commencement of services during the withdrawal period, and we begin providing the educational service, your right of withdrawal expires.
To exercise withdrawal, contact us at josef@jazykaintegrace.cz with clear statement of your decision. We will refund payments within 14 days of receiving withdrawal notice.

sections.heading: 6. Cancellation and Refunds
paragraphs: Course cancellations by participants: Full refund if canceled 7+ days before course start. Partial refunds for unused sessions may be provided at our discretion after course commencement.
Course cancellations by us: Full refund or transfer to alternative dates/courses.
Refunds are processed to the original payment method within 14 days.

sections.heading: 7. Intellectual Property
paragraphs: All course materials, content, and teaching methods are our intellectual property or used under license.
Students receive limited license to use materials for personal educational purposes only.
Recording, redistribution, or commercial use of our content is prohibited without written consent.

sections.heading: 8. Code of Conduct
paragraphs: We expect respectful, professional behavior toward instructors and fellow students.
Harassment, discrimination, disruptive behavior, or violation of course policies may result in removal without refund.
We reserve the right to refuse service to individuals who violate our community standards.

sections.heading: 9. Limitation of Liability
paragraphs: Our liability is limited to the amount paid for the specific course or service.
We are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.
Technical issues, internet connectivity problems, or force majeure events do not constitute grounds for compensation beyond course rescheduling or refund.

sections.heading: 10. Governing Law and Disputes
paragraphs: These terms are governed by Czech law. Any disputes will be resolved by Czech courts.
For consumer disputes, you may contact the Czech Trade Inspection Authority or use online dispute resolution platform at ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr.

sections.heading: 11. Contact
paragraphs: For questions about these terms, contact us at josef@jazykaintegrace.cz or +420 605 839 456.

sections.heading: 1. Data Controller
paragraphs: Jazyk a Integrace s.r.o. (IČO: 23812036) is the data controller for personal data processing.
Contact: josef@jazykaintegrace.cz, Černomořská 384/9, 101 00 Praha 10, Czech Republic.

sections.heading: 2. Personal Data We Collect
paragraphs: Registration data: Name, email address, phone number, language preferences, and learning goals.
Payment data: Billing information processed by Stripe (we don't store payment card details).
Usage data: Course progress, attendance records, and communication history.
Website data: IP address, browser information, and cookies (with your consent).

sections.heading: 3. Legal Basis and Purposes
paragraphs: Contract performance: To provide educational services, process payments, and communicate about courses.
Legitimate interest: To improve our services, prevent fraud, and maintain security.
Consent: For marketing communications and analytics cookies.
Legal obligation: To maintain accounting records and comply with tax requirements.

sections.heading: 4. Data Sharing
paragraphs: Payment processor: Stripe processes payment information securely.
Email service: We use email services to send course communications.
Analytics: PostHog analytics (only with your consent).
We do not sell personal data to third parties.

sections.heading: 5. Data Retention
paragraphs: Active accounts: Data retained while you use our services.
Billing records: 10 years for tax and accounting purposes.
Marketing data: Until you withdraw consent.
Inactive accounts: Deleted after 3 years of inactivity.

sections.heading: 6. Your Rights (GDPR)
paragraphs: Access: Request copies of your personal data.
Rectification: Correct inaccurate information.
Erasure: Delete your data (subject to legal obligations).
Portability: Receive your data in machine-readable format.
Object: Opt out of marketing or data processing based on legitimate interest.
Restrict processing: Temporarily limit how we use your data.

sections.heading: 7. Data Security
paragraphs: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your data.
All data transmission is encrypted using SSL/TLS protocols.
Access to personal data is limited to authorized personnel only.

sections.heading: 8. International Transfers
paragraphs: Your data may be processed in countries outside the EU where our service providers operate.
We ensure adequate protection through standard contractual clauses or adequacy decisions.

sections.heading: 9. Contact and Complaints
paragraphs: Exercise your rights by emailing josef@jazykaintegrace.cz.
You have the right to lodge a complaint with the Czech data protection authority (ÚOOÚ) at uoou.cz.

sections.heading: 1. What are Cookies
paragraphs: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit our website.
They help us provide you with a better experience and understand how our website is used.

sections.heading: 2. Essential Cookies
paragraphs: These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off.
They include: Session management, security features, language preferences, and login status.
These cookies do not require your consent as they are essential for service delivery.

sections.heading: 3. Analytics Cookies
paragraphs: We use PostHog analytics to understand website usage and improve user experience.
These cookies collect anonymous data about page visits, user interactions, and technical information.
We only use analytics cookies with your explicit consent.

sections.heading: 4. Managing Your Cookie Preferences
paragraphs: When you first visit our site, you'll see a cookie consent banner where you can accept or reject analytics cookies.
You can change your preferences anytime by clicking 'Cookie Preferences' in the footer.
You can also manage cookies through your browser settings, though this may affect website functionality.

sections.heading: 5. Third-Party Cookies
paragraphs: Analytics: PostHog (only with consent) - privacy policy at posthog.com/privacy.
Payments: Stripe may set cookies during checkout - privacy policy at stripe.com/privacy.
We do not use advertising or social media tracking cookies.

sections.heading: 6. Cookie Retention
paragraphs: Essential cookies expire when you close your browser or after a session timeout.
Analytics cookies are automatically deleted after 12 months.
Your consent preferences are stored for 12 months.

sections.heading: 1. Our Commitment
paragraphs: Jazyk a Integrace s.r.o. is committed to making our website accessible to all users, including those with disabilities.
We strive to meet or exceed Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA standards.

sections.heading: 2. Accessibility Features
paragraphs: Clear navigation structure and consistent layout across pages.
High contrast colors and readable fonts.
Keyboard navigation support for all interactive elements.
Alternative text for images and descriptive link text.
Form labels and error messages that work with screen readers.

sections.heading: 3. Known Limitations
paragraphs: Some third-party embedded content (payment forms, analytics) may not fully meet accessibility standards.
Video content may not have captions or audio descriptions.
PDF documents may not be fully optimized for screen readers.

sections.heading: 4. Assistive Technologies
paragraphs: Our website is tested with popular screen readers including NVDA and JAWS.
The site works with keyboard navigation and voice recognition software.
We support browser zoom up to 200% without horizontal scrolling.

sections.heading: 5. Feedback and Support
paragraphs: If you encounter accessibility barriers, please contact us immediately.
We will work to provide the information in an alternative accessible format.
Contact: josef@jazykaintegrace.cz or +420 605 839 456.

sections.heading: 6. Ongoing Improvements
paragraphs: We regularly review and update our website to improve accessibility.
We conduct periodic accessibility audits and user testing.
Staff receive training on accessibility best practices.
